raw_response: {"rating": -2.2, "lean": "Lean Left", "explanation": "This is a news report. The author’s framing consistently privileges a critical view of Trump’s economic policies through one-sided sourcing and editorial choices, leading to a Lean Left rating. The piece centers a single prominent critic without balance and includes the author’s own connective prose linking negative market expectations to Trump, supporting a Lean Left classification at -2.2. Detailed Analysis: (1) Headline and subhead frame Trump’s policies as risking stagflation and deterring investment, presented with the authority of a “top economist,” priming readers toward a negative interpretation; (2) The article relies almost entirely on one critical source (Stiglitz) and offers no countervailing perspectives from the administration or supportive economists, a structural omission that tilts left; (3) In the author’s own voice, it asserts that investors have pared back rate-cut bets since Trump’s return to office amid mounting concern over fallout from a global trade war, explicitly linking market worries to Trump; (4) An embedded editorial link titled “Forget Trump’s tariffs, the president’s bond market threat is worse” reinforces and extends a negative framing of Trump’s economic approach; (5) Authorial terminology such as “tariff threats” (outside quotations) carries a mildly pejorative tone and contributes to the critical framing without balancing language."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report. The author’s framing consistently privileges a critical view of Trump’s economic policies through one-sided sourcing and editorial choices, leading to a Lean Left rating. The piece centers a single prominent critic without balance and includes the author’s own connective prose linking negative market expectations to Trump, supporting a Lean Left classification at -2.2. Detailed Analysis: (1) Headline and subhead frame Trump’s policies as risking stagflation and deterring investment, presented with the authority of a “top economist,” priming readers toward a negative interpretation; (2) The article relies almost entirely on one critical source (Stiglitz) and offers no countervailing perspectives from the administration or supportive economists, a structural omission that tilts left; (3) In the author’s own voice, it asserts that investors have pared back rate-cut bets since Trump’s return to office amid mounting concern over fallout from a global trade war, explicitly linking market worries to Trump; (4) An embedded editorial link titled “Forget Trump’s tariffs, the president’s bond market threat is worse” reinforces and extends a negative framing of Trump’s economic approach; (5) Authorial terminology such as “tariff threats” (outside quotations) carries a mildly pejorative tone and contributes to the critical framing without balancing language.

explanation: This is a news report. The author’s framing consistently privileges a critical view of Trump’s economic policies through one-sided sourcing and editorial choices, leading to a Lean Left rating. The piece centers a single prominent critic without balance and includes the author’s own connective prose linking negative market expectations to Trump, supporting a Lean Left classification at -2.2. Detailed Analysis: (1) Headline and subhead frame Trump’s policies as risking stagflation and deterring investment, presented with the authority of a “top economist,” priming readers toward a negative interpretation; (2) The article relies almost entirely on one critical source (Stiglitz) and offers no countervailing perspectives from the administration or supportive economists, a structural omission that tilts left; (3) In the author’s own voice, it asserts that investors have pared back rate-cut bets since Trump’s return to office amid mounting concern over fallout from a global trade war, explicitly linking market worries to Trump; (4) An embedded editorial link titled “Forget Trump’s tariffs, the president’s bond market threat is worse” reinforces and extends a negative framing of Trump’s economic approach; (5) Authorial terminology such as “tariff threats” (outside quotations) carries a mildly pejorative tone and contributes to the critical framing without balancing language.
